The correct answer is \boxed{D}.

Option D includes **♭B**, which is not enharmonic to **A** (the original note in the set), while the others correctly represent the same pitch classes (E-F-A-C) in different spellings. The enharmonic equivalents must maintain the same notes, just altered in accidentals (e.g., **♭A = G#**, **♭C = B**).
